definitions.en: Important or significant; to value highly.
definitions.vi: Quan trọng hoặc có ý nghĩa; đánh giá cao.

example.vi: Anh ấy rất trọng danh dự.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He always respect the opinions of elders in the family.
She thinks that you should respect other people's feelings when talking.
I learned that you should respect your friends to keep a long-lasting friendship.
notes_md:
**trọng** = to respect, to value someone/something (a bit formal on its own).

As a verb, it’s usually followed directly by a noun, but in real life it often appears in compounds like **tôn trọng** (stronger “respect”) or **coi trọng** (value highly).  

It has a very formal/literary feel when used alone.  
Very common related words: **tôn trọng** (respect), opposite: **coi thường** (look down on).  

It can also mean “heavy/weighty” in Sino-Vietnamese words like **quan trọng** (important = weighty).
notes:
trọng = to respect, to value someone/something (a bit formal on its own).

As a verb, it’s usually followed directly by a noun, but in real life it often appears in compounds like tôn trọng (stronger “respect”) or coi trọng (value highly).  

It has a very formal/literary feel when used alone.  
Very common related words: tôn trọng (respect), opposite: coi thường (look down on).  

It can also mean “heavy/weighty” in Sino-Vietnamese words like quan trọng (important = weighty).
exampleSentences: Anh luôn trọng ý kiến của người lớn tuổi trong gia đình.
Chị nghĩ rằng cần phải trọng cảm xúc của người khác khi nói chuyện.
Em học được rằng nên trọng bạn bè để giữ tình bạn lâu dài.

content.sentence: Vấn đề này rất ___ với chúng ta.

translation.en: This issue is very important to us.
